profile.profile_text:
ALEXANDER J. NACHMAN
Port Washington, NY | (516) 998-8530 | ajn82@cornell.edu |  LinkedIn

Rising junior at Cornell University studying Industrial and Labor Relations with a concentration in Economics. Interested in how data, strategy, and storytelling connect to shape the business and media side of sports. Combines analytical thinking with clear communication and collaborative experience from marketing and outreach internships.
EDUCATION
Cornell University | School of Industrial and Labor Relations 	Ithaca, NY
Bachelor of Science in Industrial and Labor Relations – Concentration: Economics  GPA: 3.46		    Class of 2028
Coursework: Macroeconomics, Statistics & Data Science, Calculus, Introduction to Personality, Moral Dilemmas in the Law
Schreiber High School	Port Washington, NY
Weighted GPA: 4.68      ACT: 36		    Class of 2024
Awards: National Merit Scholar Finalist, AP Scholar with Distinction, National Honor Society, Foreign Language Honor Society, Mu Alpha Theta Mathematics Honor Society 
AP Coursework: Statistics, Psychology, Calculus, Biology, Physics, U.S. History, European History, French 	
INTERNSHIP EXPERIENCE
Marketing Intern | City and State Magazine – New York, NY	            Jun 2025 – Current
Partner with the VP of Digital Growth to manage and analyze prospect data, supporting outreach strategy and business development targeting nonprofit segments.
Maintain and enhance multi-year datasets for campaign targeting and audience segmentation, optimizing sales team outreach for sponsorship and award programs.
 Research Intern | City and State Magazine – New York, NY					     Jun 2023 – Aug 2023
Researched and compiled contact lists of nonprofit and public-sector organizations to support outreach, marketing, and editorial planning.
Organized and maintained internal records in Google Sheets to streamline access for sales and development teams.
Intern | NYS Congressional Voter Outreach – Great Neck, NY     			                   Sep 2022 – Nov 2022
Continued voter engagement efforts after the primary election, focusing on increasing voter turnout across key districts.
Supported regional outreach operations through phone banking, event staffing, and logistical support.
Campaign Intern | Jon Kaiman for Congress – Great Neck, NY 				     Jun 2022 – Aug 2022 
Conducted voter outreach through door-to-door canvassing and phone banking in support of campaign’s primary efforts.
Assisted in organizing fundraising events and community meet-and-greets to increase candidate visibility and engagement.
SELECTED ACADEMIC EXPERIENCE
Applied economic modeling and data analysis frameworks to evaluate market behavior and global trends.
Used R and RStudio in Statistics and Data Science coursework to perform regression analysis and interpret real-world datasets.
Strengthened analytical writing and critical reasoning through coursework in Moral Dilemmas in the Law and Personality Psychology.
ADDITIONAL EXPERIENCE
Golf Attendant | Village Club of Sands Point – Port Washington, NY  	Jun 2023 – Aug 2023
Helped maintain the driving range and organized golf equipment to support day-to-day club operations.
Golf Caddy | North Hempstead Country Club – Port Washington, NY  	   Jun 2021 – Aug 2021	
Assisted golfers during rounds by carrying clubs, providing yardage estimates, and helping locate golf balls.
LEADERSHIP & INVOLVEMENT
Cornell Sports Business Society | Member - Engages with professionals/case studies on sports media and event management.
Cornell University Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity | Member 
Sigma Nu Fraternity  | Social Chairman

jobText:
About the job
Company Description

Digitas is the Networked Experience Agency, built on the vision that we create magnetic experiences that earn the right for brands to exist in human networks. Today, and tomorrow. We deliver Networked Experiences by leveraging comprehensive data, technology, creative, media and strategy capabilities. Digitas delivers ambitious outcomes via unique solutions that include Creative Experiences, Integrated Media, Addressable Relationships, Social Marketing and Total Commerce. Celebrated by AdAge as Data and Insights Agency of the Year, U.S Campaign’s Brand Experience Agency of the Year, Media Network of the Year and celebrated by Forrester and Gartner, Digitas serves the world’s leading brands through a global network comprised of more than 5,500 employees across over 65 offices in 43 countries.

The Digitas culture is made up of fearless, inventive and generous Unicorns of all kinds.

Overview

As an Associate, Strategy intern, you’ll support the agency’s core strategic disciplines, including account planning, primary research, media planning and analytics. You’ll rotate through disciplines, gaining exposure to the full range of tools and techniques needed to develop breakthrough insight and results-generating marketing programs.

Responsibilities

Overview of a day in the life: 

Using data-supported insights to design strategies that put the consumer at the center
Applying psychology, anthropology and behavioral economics principles to solve complex marketing challenges
Designing communications strategies to bring modern media architectures to life
Working collaboratively with other capabilities, including Data & Analysis, Media and Creative, to work out solutions for clients
Briefing creative teams for a range of assignments within the agency
Researching, both qualitatively and quantitatively to influence major project directions and decisions
Studying the consumer’s environment in order to define the experience a brand needs to provide people and what problem it will solve for them
Tracking the activities of brands and understanding the competitive landscape
Keeping teams abreast of pop culture and trends and the implications they have on our brands and businesses
Being an indispensable source of information, insight and inspiration in the creative process to enhance the work
Tapping into diverse data sources and working within tools such as Comscore, MRI, Global Web Index, and Google Analytics

Qualifications

Traits and skills we’re looking for:

Constantly observes and questions the world around you with an insatiable curiosity for how people think, feel and behave
Is excited by the thought of uncovering opportunities that move our brands businesses forward
Stays privy to new digital platforms, social media, technologies and trends
Is passionate about understanding culture through mediums like podcasts, TED talks, and books, etc. (Bonus points if you’ve read books by Byron Sharp or Daniel Kahneman)
Interested in behavioral economics, social sciences, decision-making, cognitive biases/heuristics, psychology, etc.
Looks for creative ways to reframe problems into exciting new challenges
Isn’t afraid to have a strong POV and can present it clearly, simply and persuasively
Works well as part of a team, sometimes leading, sometimes following and supporting
A lifelong learner who is never satisfied with one’s knowledge and skills of their craft-not afraid to challenge the status quo
Self-motivated, eager to jump into any challenge
To be considered, candidates will need to submit a YOUnicorn Brief as part of the application. Briefs should be no more than 60 seconds.
Familiarity with prompt-based interaction and commonly used generative AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Google Gemini, DALL·E, Midjourney) is a plus, especially for tasks like ideation, research, or content generation.

Got the sparkle? Apply if you believe your unique skills are a fit.

Show us what makes you a Unicorn!

Please create a short video or presentation showing us your true Unicorn self to include in your application submission, using the instructions below. You do not need to be in the content you create, this is an opportunity to get creative, show us what makes you unique by highlighting things we might not see on your resume.
